title: Double steerable sheath and method for deployment of a medical device
abstract: The present invention relates to a double steerable sheath, a delivery system comprising such a sheath and a method for deployment of a medical device, e.g. a replacement heart valve prosthesis, in a patient's heart.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A steerable sheath comprising an elongate sheath, at least one actuator, at least two different sheath steering points, means for connecting the at least one actuator with the at least two different sheath steering points. 2. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 comprising two, three or four actuators, preferably wherein one actuator is independently connected with one sheath steering point or wherein two independent actuators actuate the at least two steering points independently. 3. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the actuator is combined with a proximal handle. 4. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the at least two sheath steering points are located distal . 5. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the elongate sheath is made of or comprises a combination of materials selected from biocompatible materials, Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE), polymers with differing durometers, , stainless steel, nitinol, PEBAXÂ®, or/and a polyamide, e.g. GrilamidÂ®. 6. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein a softer material is used on the distal end of the sheath and harder material on the proximal side of the sheath, preferably stainless steel, PEBAXÂ®, preferably GrilamidÂ®. 7. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 comprising two actuators, two sheath steering points, two means for connecting each of the 8. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the means for connecting is a wire, e.g. a flat wire, a round wire, a cable, a filament, or a tissue. 9. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the means for connecting can be pulled proximally by way of the actuator(s). 10. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the at least two different sheath steering points are spaced circumferential around the sheath or/and longitudinally along the sheath. 11. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 comprising or exhibiting two, three, or four sheath steering points, preferably two sheath steering points. 12. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the at least two or two different sheath steering points are spaced in an angle of 10Â°, 20Â°, 30Â°, 45Â°, 90Â°, 120Â°, 140Â°, 160Â°, or 180Â°. 13. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the at least two or two different sheath steering points are spaced with a distance of 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, or 10 cm. 14. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the at least two or two different sheath steering points are spaced in a combination of the angle according to claim 12 and the distance according to claim 13. 15. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the two different sheath steering points are located in an angle of 180Â° or in an angle of 180Â° and with a spacing of 5 to 10 cm, preferably 7 or 8 cm. 16. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein the two different sheath steering points are counter-acting movable by way of the two actuators. 17. A steerable sheath according to claim 1 wherein two counteracting actuators allow to reach various target points within one horizontal plane by adjusting both bends by way of two actuators. 18. A catheter system for deployment of a heart valve prosthesis comprising a steerable sheath according to claims 1 to 17. 19. A method for deployment of a heart valve prosthesis wherein a defined catheter point is actuated to a defined target point using a steerable sheath according to any of claims 1 to 17. 20. A method according to claim 19 wherein the actuators are counter-actuated in order to reach various target points within one horizontal plane by adjusting each of the actuators in counter- direction. 21. A method according to claim 20 wherein the actuators are counter-actuated to effect essentially a S-shape of the sheath. 22. A method according to claim 19 to 21 wherein the target point comprises the prosthesis in the catheter perpendicular to the implantation level of the implantation site. 23. A method according to claim 19 to 22 wherein the heart valve prosthesis to be deployed is deployed in the native annulus essentially central and/or essentially perpendicular with regard to the native annulus level. 24. A method for deployment of a heart valve prosthesis wherein the heart valve prosthesis is deployed using a steerable sheath according to any of claims 1 to 17 or/and a catheter system according to any of claim 18.
